title: Freezing of hPSCs grown on MEFs
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"9fb9j","text":"This protocol describes the standard procedure of freezing human pluripotent stem cells (hPSCs), which were grown on inactivated mouse embryonic fibroblasts (MEFs).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cdtr4","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cfts9","text":"General notes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1hq5r","text":"1. Throughout this protocol, the term hPSC is used to collectively refer to both hiPSCs and hESCs. All described procedures have been tested and work equally well for hiPSCs and hESCs.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4lk4u","text":"2. Until otherwise indicated, hPSCs are routinely grown in a humidified cell culture incubator under “low” oxygen conditions. We have successfully maintained hPSCs using either 3% O2 (3% O2, 5% CO2) or 5% O2 (5% O2, 5% CO2) conditions. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"73qm0","text":"3. While freezing hPSCs as single cell solution (using Rock Inhibitor) results in better cell recovery, some laboratories prefer freezing of hPSCs as cell clusters. We have used both approaches and do not observe obvious differences. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: When hPSCs reach 50% confluency (usually on day 6 from last passage), change medium to 3 ml hPSCs medium + Rock inhibitor, to prepare for freezing the next day.
Before starting: a. Prepare Freezing Medium I and II and keep on ice. b. Pre-label appropriate number of cryovials (freeze approx. 2 vials/well of a 6-well plate)
Freezing Medium I %s %s hESCs medium 5 ml FB essence 5 ml Freezing Medium II %s %s FB essence 8 ml DMSO 2 ml
Wash hPSCs 1x with DPBS
Add 0.5 ml Trypsin to each well
Incubate 00:05:00 37 °C in incubator
Gently shake the plate
Check under microscope to confirm all cells have been lifted from the plate.
Add 2 ml Wash Medium to each well to inactivate trypsin
Triturate using P1000 tips and collect all cell suspension to a 15 ml conical tube
Centrifuge at 200-300 x g, °C, 5 minutes
Aspirate supernatant
Gently re-suspend the pellet in 500 µl Freezing Medium I per vial to be frozen.
In each cryovial, add 500 µl pre-chilled Freezing Medium II.
Dispense 500 µl cell suspension into each cryovial using P1000, mix
Temporarily keep cryovials On ice until all cells dispensed
Place cryovials into Styrofoam microtube freezer box or pre-cooled (4°C on ice) NALGENE™ Cryo 1°C Freezing Container filled with 250 ml of Isopropanol.
Freeze at -80°C Overnight
For long term storage, store cryovials in liquid nitrogen (-196ºC).
Before starting: a. Prepare Freezing Medium I and II and keep on ice. b. Pre-label appropriate number of cryovials (freeze approx. 2 vials/well of a 6 well plate)
Wash hPSCs (on feeders) 1x with PBS.
Use 1 ml Collagenase Solution/well of a 6-well plate.
Incubate 00:45:00 37 °C . Watch for edge curling of the colonies as this indicates that collagenase incubation is complete.
Add 3 ml hPSC medium to quench collagenase
Pipette repeatedly with 5 ml pipette to lift colonies, careful not to carry over too many MEFs.
Collect into 15 ml conical tube.
Add 4-5 ml Wash Medium.
Gravity precipitate cells 00:10:00
Reduce volume to 1-2 ml

Using a 10 ml strip pipette, triturate the colonies 5-10 times against the bottom of the tube to break up cell clusters a. The objective is to reduce cluster size, not to completely dissociate to single cells. b. Avoid introducing air bubbles.
Gravity precipitate cells 00:10:00
Resuspend cells in 10 ml hPSC medium
Pellet the cells at 200 x g, Room temperature, 5 minutes aspirate the supernatant
Gently re-suspend the pellet in 500 µl Freezing Medium I per vial to be frozen.
Carefully add 500 µl Freezing Medium II per vial to be frozen.
Dispense 1 ml aliquots in pre-labeled cryovials and keep On ice
Place cryovials into Styrofoam microtube freezer box or pre-cooled (4°C on ice) NALGENE™ Cryo 1°C Freezing Container filled with 250 ml of Isopropanol.
Place container in a -80°C Freezer Overnight
After an overnight incubation, cryovials are removed from the Freezing Container and placed in Liquid Nitrogen (-196ºC) for long-term storage.

hPSCs Medium %s %s DMEM/F12 385 ml Fetal Bovine Serum (FBS) 75 ml Knockout Serum Replacement 25 ml L-Glutamine (100X) 5 ml Penicillin & Streptomycin (100X) 5 ml MEM Non-Essential Amino Acids (100X) 5 ml 2-Mercaptoethanol (10,000X) 50 ul Heat Stable Recombinant Human FGF2 (25ug/ml)* 80 ul L-Glutamine (100X) %s %s L-Glutamine, powder 14.6 g MilliQ H2O 500 ml 2-Mercaptoethanol (10,000X) %s %s 2-Mercaptoethanol 0.78 ml MilliQ H2O 9.22 ml Heat Stable Recombinant Human FGF2 (25µg/ml) %s %s Heat Stable Recombinant Human FGF2 500 ug 0.1% BSA 20 ml hPSCs Medium + Rock Inhibitor %s %s hESCs medium 500 ml Y-27632 (1,000X) 500 ul Y-27632 (1,000X) %s %s Y-27632 5 mg DMSO 1.56 ml
Wash medium %s %s DMEM/F12 470 ml Newborn Calf Serum 25 ml Penicillin & Streptomycin (100X) 5 ml

Collagenase solution %s %s Collagenase type IV 10 mg KSR medium 10 ml KSR medium %s %s DMEM/F12 385 ml Knockout Serum Replacement 100 ml L-Glutamine (200 mM) 5 ml Penicillin & Streptomycin (100X) 5 ml MEM Non-Essential Amino Acids (100X) 5 ml
